title: Browser Memory Daemon
lead: A local-first browser-memory stack: Windows Chrome captures visible page text, a WSL daemon stores it in SQLite + FTS5, and the operator searches, inspects, and forgets it locally.
sub: The default `all` mode maximizes personal recall with no daemon redaction or built-in URL filtering, while explicit local block rules, loopback auth, and WSL runtime boundaries keep the system operator-owned.
features_intro: The repo is a complete local browser-memory implementation, not a mockup: capture, policy, storage, search, UI, deletion, media sidecars, and real Chrome verification are all present.

features.title: Text-first recall
features.body: Visible Chrome page text becomes documents, visits, snapshots, chunks, and FTS rows in WSL. Text recall does not wait on media bytes.

features.title: Exact local search
features.body: SQLite FTS5 backs search, timeline, recent captures, document detail, snapshot text, and source snippets through the CLI, API, and local UI.

features.title: Adjustable policy modes
features.body: `all`, `recall`, `balanced`, and `strict` are implemented. `all` removes built-in URL filtering/redaction but still honors explicit local block rules.

features.title: Durable media sidecars
features.body: Browser IndexedDB queues, raw blob upload, X/Twitter CDP recovery, daemon public fetch, HLS handling, rolling cache gates, and bounded automatic spool recovery preserve media provenance without blocking text capture.

features.title: Operator surfaces
features.body: A token-bootstrapped local dashboard, CLI, popup/options controls, HTTP API, doctor checks, policy rules, and deletion receipts cover day-to-day operation.

features.title: Real browser verification
features.body: The test suite includes daemon pytest, extension node:test/build, and Chrome for Testing e2e for capture/search/forget/media/lifecycle behavior.

flow_intro: The runtime spine is simple: capture fast, classify locally, store text durably, fetch media lazily, and keep every operator read/delete path authenticated.

flow.title: Chrome observes the page
flow.body: Content scripts extract visible text, media refs, scroll/dwell hints, and lifecycle signals while retaining hidden/form/editable/script/style/no-script skips.

flow.title: Service worker owns transport
flow.body: The extension service worker attaches the bearer token, queues capture/media work, and posts only to the loopback WSL daemon.

flow.title: Policy classifies locally
flow.body: The daemon applies the selected policy mode plus persistent block rules; non-all modes redact before storage.

flow.title: Text is stored first
flow.body: The ingest path normalizes URLs, hashes text snapshots, writes complete cleaned text, provenance, chunks, and FTS rows to SQLite, then returns stable IDs.

flow.title: Media sidecars fill in
flow.body: Browser and daemon workers fetch/upload blobs asynchronously under per-artifact, snapshot, domain, and global cache gates.

flow.title: Operator reads or forgets
flow.body: CLI/API/UI surfaces expose exact search, recent/timeline/detail, doctor diagnostics, media cache controls, policy rules, and forget receipts.

model_intro: One mental model: Chrome is the sensor, WSL is the memory, and every retrieval/deletion path stays local and token-gated.
model_cap: Chrome captures; WSL remembers. Media sidecars are asynchronous; exact text recall remains the source of truth.
commands_intro: The operator surface covers daemon health, exact recall, policy and deletion, media/cache recovery, passive export, and dry-run-first backup/restore.

commands.touches: Loopback API, DB readiness, runtime diagnostics
SQLite/FTS exact search and detail views
Persistent local block rules in every policy mode
Documents, visits, SQLite text/chunks/FTS, legacy derivatives, media rows/blobs, receipts
Daemon-public fetch tasks, blob cache, purge/rehydrate controls
Guarded-root recovery, bounded spool transfer, durable tombstone convergence
Query-only body-safe observation pages and manifest-verified local bundles
WSL user services, Windows extension copy, Chrome for Testing e2e

audiences_intro: Three doors in, depending on the reader's altitude.

audiences.title: Executive Brief
audiences.body: What it does, why it matters, maturity, and safety posture without implementation detail.

audiences.title: User Guide
audiences.body: Daily-driver setup, state checks, search, policy rules, media cache controls, and troubleshooting.

audiences.title: Architecture
audiences.body: Requirements trace, C4 atlas, behavioral diagrams, ADRs, API/CLI contracts, and verification gates.

boundary_intro: The product is powerful because it captures broadly by default. The safety story is locality, explicit operator control, and no hidden cloud path.

inside.title: SQLite + FTS5
inside.sub:
complete text + refs
media cache + spool

boundary_cap: Runtime DB/state belongs under WSL XDG paths; blobs can live under a configured WSL-visible NAS root. The repo contains source, docs, generated visuals, and tests — not browser memory, tokens, cookies, or raw captures.
boundary_note: The default all mode is an operator-selected recall posture: no daemon redaction and no built-in URL filtering. Explicit local block rules still apply. See security-model for the full contract.
docmap_intro: This page is the visual front door. Markdown stays canonical; the linked HTML pages are generated companions for polished browser reading.
footer_provenance: Metrics grounded in checked-in source: 336 static test functions across 60 files from scripts/generate_test_inventory.py; 21 top-level CLI commands from cli.py; 22 route descriptors from routes.py; 26 C4 .mmd views generated from docs/architecture/workspace.dsl; zero cloud runtime dependencies in current architecture docs/code.
